page.content: The first table presents data categorized under "Category" and "Number," with "Patient cognition" at 23, "Medicine safety" at 25, "Involvement strategy" at 9, "Medical staff-related" at 11, culminating in a "Total" of 68. The second table outlines various "Parameter" metrics across three strata, detailing coefficients (β), standard errors (SE), and P values.

page.content: For the first stratum, "Age (years)" shows β at 0.069, SE at 0.039, and P value as n.s.; "Male: 1, female: 0" has β at 0.083, SE at 0.754, and P value as n.s.; "SBP (mmHg)" has β at 0.045, SE at 0.017, and P value as n.s.; "HbA1c (%)" reports β at 0.272, SE at 0.165, with a P value <0.001; "Log10 AER (μg/min)" indicates β at 0.185, SE at 0.485, and P value <0.001; "Retinopathy: 1, None: 0" shows β at 0.085, SE at 0.730, and P value as n.s.; "Hemoglobin (g/L)" has β at −0.086, SE at 0.269, and P value as n.s. In the second stratum, "Age (years)" has β at 0.153, SE at 0.153, and P value <0.001; "Male: 1, female: 0" shows β at 0.119, SE at 0.381, and P value <0.01; "SBP (mmHg)" has β at 0.100, SE at 0.010, with P value <0.01; "HbA1c (%)" reports β at 0.236, SE at 0.091, with P value <0.001; "Log10 AER (μg/min)" shows β at 0.195, SE at 0.246, with P value <0.001; "Retinopathy: 1, None: 0" has β at 0.054, SE at 0.357, P value as n.s.; "Hemoglobin (g/L)" reports β at −0.071, SE at 0.132, and P value as n.s. In the third stratum, "Age (years)" has β at −0.071, SE at 0.040, with P value as n.s.; "Male: 1, female: 0" shows β at 0.181, SE at 0.726, P value <0.05; "SBP (mmHg)" has β at 0.128, SE at 0.017, with P value as n.s.; "HbA1c (%)" shows β at 0.128, SE at 0.170, with P value as n.s.; "Log10 AER (μg/min)" indicates β at 0.516, SE at 0.388, with P value <0.001; "Retinopathy: 1, None: 0" shows β at −0.075, SE at 0.647, with P value as n.s.; "Hemoglobin (g/L)" has β at −0.249, SE at 0.201, and P value <0.01.
